_note: First scenario/prompt-fixture skill (input is a feature ticket, not code). Rubric from sentinel/fixtures/test-plan/expected-findings.md against discount-code.md. The signature failure is the green-light plan: happy-path-only with loose, un-testable criteria (the thing the SKILL warns against — 'find bugs, not describe how to make a green light'). The one structural token is an Unhappy Paths section (a plan without one is definitionally incomplete). must_surface asserts specific edge/unhappy cases + layer discipline; each is one groundable proposition (the compound-claim lesson from #81/#84). `anchors` exist only for the offline heuristic judge; the LLM judge grades `claim` directly. Live path pastes the ticket into $ARGUMENTS; offline --self-test grades the samples.

must_surface.claim: Surfaces the expired-code edge case — a code applied at or after its expiry timestamp is rejected, with the boundary called out at exactly expiry.
Surfaces the already-redeemed single-use unhappy path — the same account cannot redeem a code twice (no double discount).
Assigns a `unit` layer to the pure discount-amount math (percentage / fixed / the $0 clamp) rather than labelling it `e2e` — layer discipline.

must_not.claim: Must NOT write loose, un-testable acceptance criteria ("works correctly", "handled gracefully", "as expected") — a criterion must be able to fail if the behavior is wrong.
Must NOT invent tests for behavior the ticket puts out of scope — creating/administering codes is a separate flow, not part of this plan.
